description: Collection of toggles with either single-select (type="single", role="radiogroup" with radio items) or multi-select (type="multiple", role="group" with pressed buttons) semantics. Radix implements roving tabindex so the whole group is one tab stop and arrow keys move between items.

keyboard.action: Moves focus into the selected item (single mode) or the first item (multiple mode), and out of the group.
In single mode, moves selection between items (wraps by default). In multiple mode, moves focus without toggling.
Same as Left / Right when orientation="vertical".
Moves to the first / last item.
In multiple mode, toggles the focused item.

focus.notes: Each item uses focus-visible:ring-1 via toggleVariants. Only one item in the group is a tab stop at a time (roving tabindex).

announcements: Single mode announces as a radio group with the selected item's label.
Multiple mode announces as a group; each item announces with pressed / not pressed state.
screenReader.notes: Context provides size / variant to children so consumers don't have to pass them on each item. Icon-only items still need an aria-label.

contrast.text: Item text uses foreground; pressed state uses text-accent-foreground on bg-accent. Both meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Focus ring uses ring-ring; outline variant adds border-input. Both meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Only a color transition on hover / state change; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Provide an aria-label or aria-labelledby on ToggleGroup describing what's being toggled (e.g. "Text alignment").
Provide aria-label on icon-only items; the variant styling alone isn't enough for AT.
Pick type="single" for mutually-exclusive options (e.g. alignment left vs. center vs. right) and type="multiple" for independent toggles (e.g. text styling like bold, italic, underline).
